results.title: Swami Shyam
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Our Asian neighbours: India”.
A guru and his followers live in an ashram in Kulu Valley, within the Himalayas. The audience is directly involved in experiencing an Indian swami. The film ends with one of the swami’s three minute lessons on meditation. Suitable for upper primary and lower secondary levels.

results.title: Rana
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Our Asian neighbours: India”.
The story of a young Muslim girl student living in a crowded section of Old Delhi. The customary veiling of women (Purdah) and impending marriage by arrangement are examined.

results.title: Walter Spiel
results.creator_credit: James Fitzpatrick and United States Information Agency
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “One Man”.
A study of the 62 year old news vendor who sells American and foreign newspapers and magazines at his stand on a Los Angeles street corner. The camera establishes the relationship between “Willy” and his consumers as they come to his stand and look through their choices before purchase.

results.title: Questions
results.creator_credit: Bob Kingsbury, Bruce Moir and Film Australia
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Our Asian neighbours: India”.
The “God rich colony” is a huge industrial estate on the outskirts of Bombay. It is a self-contained company town where everything is provided for the factory workers and their families, including schools. An Australian director and a group of 15 year-old school children have a relaxed discussion about their concept of the Australian way of life and their lives in India.

results.title: Puppeteer
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Our Asian neighbours: India”.
Depicts the modified way of life of a traditional Rajasthan puppeteer in Vdaipur today. Living in a tent on a street corner, Bansilal works in a superb Maharajah’s Palace, now converted to a five star hotel.

results.title: Reverend Leon Sullivan
results.creator_credit: Harold Mayer and United States Information Agency
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “One Man”.
Through his inspired leadership, the Rev. Sullivan has been able to redeem and transform thousands of school dropouts and potential criminals. Describes the Opportunities Industrialisation Centre which he organised in order to train people of the ghetto to become skilled workers. Narrated by Barry Sullivan.

results.title: Bombay movies
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Our Asian neighbours: India”.
Vinod Kharna is one of the most popular Hindi movie stars in Bombay’s film industry. This film explores his life and work as a way of gaining insight into Indian “pop culture”.

results.title: Lyn McLain
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “One Man”.
Lyn McLain is the founder and director of the District of Columbia Youth Symphony Orchestra, one of the most extraordinary musical groups in the United States. It is made up of children, aged from eight to seventeen years, from all walks of life, who voluntarily give up their Saturdays to study music.
